title: Whipple-Scrumptious Roald Dahl Day!
story: Every September 13, kids and grown-ups around the world celebrate the birthday of Roald Dahl, the author who gave us Charlie and the Chocolate Factory, Matilda, and The BFG! title: Stravinsky's Sacred Song Echoes in Venice
story: Music filled the air as Igor Stravinsky's new composition, 'Canticum Sacrum,' made its grand debut. On September 13, 1956, the sacred choral work echoed through Venice's historic St. Mark's Basilica. Stravinsky, a famous Russian-born composer, crafted this piece to honor the city's rich musical heritage.
activity_question: Where did Stravinsky's 'Canticum Sacrum' premiere?

title: A Farewell to Mother Teresa
story: Crowds filled the streets of Calcutta as India bid farewell to Mother Teresa. On September 13, 1997, the world-renowned nun received a state funeral, an honor usually reserved for national leaders. People from all walks of life came to pay their respects to the 'Saint of the Gutters' who had dedicated her life to serving the poor.
activity_question: What honor did Mother Teresa receive on September 13, 1997?
follow_up_question: Why was Mother Teresa called the 'Saint of the Gutters'?

title: A Trailblazer in New Zealand Politics
story: Elizabeth McCombs shattered a glass ceiling in 1933. On September 13, she became New Zealand's first female Member of Parliament. Her victory marked a huge step for women in politics. McCombs had fought for years to give women a voice in government. Now, she could use her own voice to make real changes for her country.
activity_question: What did Elizabeth McCombs become in 1933?
activity_choices: New Zealand's first female Member of Parliament
New Zealand's first female Prime Minister
activity_answer: New Zealand's first female Member of Parliament
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for women to be involved in government?

title: Trailblazing Senator Breaks Barriers
story: Margaret Chase Smith shattered glass ceilings in 1948. On September 13, she became the first woman elected to serve in both houses of the United States Congress. As a Republican from Maine, Smith won her Senate seat, adding to her previous experience in the House of Representatives. Her groundbreaking achievement opened doors for future female leaders in American politics.
activity_question: What political milestone did Margaret Chase Smith achieve?
activity_choices: First woman elected to serve in both houses of Congress
First female President of the United States
First woman to run for Senate
First Republican woman in the House
activity_answer: First woman elected to serve in both houses of Congress
follow_up_question: Can you name the two houses of the United States Congress?

title: Asteroid Victoria Spotted in Space
story: Stargazers, get ready! In 1850, astronomer J R Hind made an exciting discovery in the night sky. He spotted a new asteroid floating in the main asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter. This space rock was named 12 Victoria, joining the family of celestial objects orbiting our sun. On September 13, Hind's sharp eyes and powerful telescope revealed this hidden gem of the cosmos.
activity_question: What did J R Hind discover in 1850?
follow_up_question: Where is the main asteroid belt located?

title: Cabral's Epic Voyage to India
story: Sails billowed as Pedro Álvares Cabral's fleet reached Calicut, India in 1500. This Portuguese explorer had embarked on a daring journey across vast oceans. On September 13, after months at sea, Cabral and his crew finally spotted the bustling port city. Their arrival marked a significant moment in the Age of Discovery, opening new trade routes between Europe and Asia.
activity_question: What city did Cabral's expedition reach?
bonus_fact: Cabral's voyage to India accidentally led to the discovery of Brazil earlier that year.

title: A Mighty Ship's Capture Changes History
story: In 1613, a massive trading ship called the Rahimi sailed the seas. It belonged to the powerful Mughal Empress Mariam-uz-Zamani. But on September 13, Portuguese sailors seized the Rahimi and its many passengers. This bold move backfired on the Portuguese. It led to a big shift in power, putting a check on their control of ocean trade routes.
activity_question: What was the name of the captured trading ship?
bonus_fact: The Rahimi was the largest Mughal trading ship of its time.

title: Byzantine Victory Against the Vandals
story: Swords clashed and shields clanged as General Belisarius led his Byzantine army to victory. In 533, on September 13, he defeated Gelimer and the Vandals at the Battle of Ad Decimium. This fierce clash took place near Carthage in North Africa, marking a significant triumph for the Byzantine Empire.
activity_question: Who did General Belisarius defeat in battle?
follow_up_question: Where was the Battle of Ad Decimium fought?
